title: BS in Data Science and Philosophy - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
BS in Data Science and Philosophy - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
BS in Data Science and Philosophy
Apply now
Request info
The Data Science and Philosophy combined major (Khoury College and the College of Social Sciences and Humanities) will prepare you with a strong foundation in formal logic — including logical proofs and the ability to represent and evaluate arguments — and understanding of the intersection of logic and data. Build exceptional oral and written communication skills, gain experience working with data in its various forms, and explore the ethical and social issues related to data.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program details
Career prospects
Career prospects
In this program, you will build a unique skill set that includes analytical thinking, ethical reasoning, and a deep understanding of the implications of data-driven decisions. Some career prospects include:
Ethics and compliance analyst
Data ethicist
AI and machine learning consultant
Data governance policy analyst
Data privacy advocate
Co-ops and internships
Co-ops and internships
During your co-op experiences, you’ll have the opportunity to apply your classroom knowledge and build connections in industry settings. Recent co-ops include:
Program operations co-op at Enel X
Cryptokernel developer at MIT Media Lab
Quality assurance co-op at Mullen Advertising Inc.
Learn more about co-ops
Research opportunities
Research opportunities
Explore the big questions related to the field as you partner with leading professors on active research projects. Our research areas include:
Artificial intelligence
Human-computer interaction
Security and privacy
Explore research at Khoury College
Learning outcomes
Learning outcomes
In this program, you’ll learn to:
Apply design principles in the construction of software systems of varying complexity.
Use current techniques, skills, and tools necessary for effective and secure computing practice.
Apply data science theory, methods, and tools to translate data into clear, actionable insights.
Campus location
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus
Applications and admissions
Deadlines
Deadlines
Learn more about our undergraduate early action, early decision, and regular decision options.
Explore deadlines
Degree costs
Degree costs
Pursuing a world-class education is an investment in your future. It’s also one of the most important decisions you’ll ever make. Visit our Undergraduate Admissions site to find current Northeastern University costs. You can also estimate the cost of your degree using our College Cost Estimator or Net Price Calculator.
Find and estimate degree costs
Financial aid and scholarships
Financial aid and scholarships
Northeastern University offers a range of financial aid and scholarship opportunities to help you fund your education. You’ll receive an initial financial aid package that outlines your options after admission. Once you enroll, Khoury College has multiple scholarship opportunities for current students to continue supporting your studies. We also encourage all students to submit the FAFSA to determine your eligibility for federal aid and loans.
Learn more about scholarship opportunities
Have questions?
Have questions?
Let us know if you have any questions about the application or enrollment process. We are here to help!
Contact an enrollment counselor
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
Wolfgang Gatterbauer
Wolfgang Gatterbauer is an associate professor at Khoury College. He works on the theory of scalable data management, with the goal of expanding data management systems and enabling them to support novel functionalities.
Read bio
Visit the College of Social Sciences and Humanities
The BS in Data Science and Philosophy program is an interdisciplinary collaboration with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities.
Learn about the College of Social Sciences and Humanities
Get a head start on your graduate degree
In our accelerated master’s programs, you can start earning your master’s degree while you are still pursuing your undergraduate studies.
Browse accelerated BS+MS programs
